key_assumptions: PMS/booking-engine integration is feasible within 30–45 days without paid custom work or long vendor queues.
Direct-booking uplift can be attributed credibly (cross-device, channel manager noise controlled) and audited inside the PMS/BE.
The GM/owner team and Head of Revenue have bandwidth to action weekly recommendations during shoulder seasons.
Swiss/GDPR-compliant hosting and data processing can be satisfied without lengthy legal delays.
Seasonality (Zermatt winter/summer peaks) will not mask a 90-day pilot’s ROI signal.
Budget can be reallocated from existing review/compset tools or agency time without political friction.
A relevant Swiss premium/luxury reference can be produced to de-risk the decision.
gm.most_fragile_assumption: Attribution credibility of direct-booking uplift (airtight, PMS/BE-auditable within a 90-day window).
gm.strongest_wedge: Swiss-hosted 90-day pilot that replaces manual weekly reporting with a PMS/booking-engine–integrated commercial cockpit proving direct-booking attribution.
gm.strongest_blocker: Unproven direct-booking attribution and PMS/booking-engine integration access/timelines.
gm.recommended_outreach_angle: Propose a Swiss-hosted 90-day pilot scheduled for a shoulder month, priced in the CHF 1.5–2k band with monthly opt-out. Commit to Day-0 integration plan with their PMS/booking engine, an attribution blueprint (UTMs, BE event tracking, holdout/A-B on key website modules), and weekly 30‑minute revenue standups. Show a live dashboard demo plus one Swiss luxury reference, and use an ROI calculator tailored to Zermatt seasonality (focus on peak weeks). Define success as ≥5% direct revenue uplift and replacement of current manual/compset workflows; offer to offset part of fees via performance milestones if attribution is met.
gm.canonical_deal_killer: No reliable PMS/booking-engine integration for direct booking attribution.

key_assumptions: A reliable PMS/booking‑engine integration is feasible within 4–8 weeks without disrupting live booking flows
There is at least CHF 1.5–2k/month that can be reallocated from agency/tools/manual time to fund a pilot
Seasonality effects can be controlled by measuring uplift across a shoulder→peak window with clean attribution
The small commercial team can action weekly recommendations if white‑glove support is provided
Owner approval can be won with a tightly scoped pilot, Swiss hosting assurances, and local references
Existing compset/reputation tools are partial and can be consolidated without losing valued functions
commercial.most_fragile_assumption: That a 10% uplift in direct booking revenue can be proven and attributed within 3 months despite Zermatt’s seasonality and small (55‑room) sample size
commercial.strongest_wedge: A 90‑day, Swiss‑hosted pilot timed for shoulder season that delivers weekly revenue actions and clean direct‑revenue attribution, replacing fragmented tools and agency tasks
commercial.strongest_blocker: Owner veto on recurring SaaS without multi‑season proof and clear integration feasibility
commercial.recommended_outreach_angle: Propose a Swiss‑hosted, 90‑day pilot priced in the CHF 1.5–2k/month band, scheduled for shoulder season to avoid peak‑winter disruption. Lead with: (1) confirmed PMS/booking‑engine integration for their stack, (2) weekly action engine focused on high‑ADR ski/wellness dates, (3) direct‑revenue attribution with baseline vs. test periods, and (4) references from Swiss alpine peers. Offer white‑glove onboarding, an annual option with discount after pilot, and a monthly opt‑out. Make the owner’s risk minimal: show an ROI calculator using their 55 rooms and ADR ~CHF 800, commit to GDPR/Swiss hosting, and define success as multi‑season proof to unlock any higher tier.
commercial.canonical_deal_killer: No reliable PMS/booking-engine integration (cannot ingest real-time bookings/rates)

merged.primary_wedge: A 90‑day, Swiss‑hosted pilot timed for shoulder season that delivers weekly revenue actions and clean direct‑revenue attribution, replacing fragmented tools and agency tasks
merged.primary_blocker: Unproven direct-booking attribution and PMS/booking-engine integration access/timelines.
merged.outreach_angle: Propose a Swiss-hosted 90-day pilot scheduled for a shoulder month, priced in the CHF 1.5–2k band with monthly opt-out. Commit to Day-0 integration plan with their PMS/booking engine, an attribution blueprint (UTMs, BE event tracking, holdout/A-B on key website modules), and weekly 30‑minute revenue standups. Show a live dashboard demo plus one Swiss luxury reference, and use an ROI calculator tailored to Zermatt seasonality (focus on peak weeks). Define success as ≥5% direct revenue uplift and replacement of current manual/compset workflows; offer to offset part of fees via performance milestones if attribution is met.
merged.deal_killer: No reliable PMS/booking-engine integration for direct booking attribution.
disagreements: deal_killer: GM="No reliable PMS/booking-engine integration for direct booking attribution." vs CM="No reliable PMS/booking-engine integration (cannot ingest real-time bookings/rates)"

key_assumptions: GM can secure owner approval for any recurring spend above CHF 1.5–2k/month within a single cycle
PMS and booking engine support timely two-way integration without material extra vendor fees or long queues
Direct-booking uplift of ~10% is attributable within 3–6 months with current tracking/attribution fidelity
Small 49-room team can absorb weekly action workflows without adding headcount
Swiss/GDPR-compliant hosting and a comparable Swiss reference are available to de-risk procurement
Brand/Swiss Quality network will not block third‑party integrations or require lengthy central approvals
Seasonality (Chur winter/summer peaks) will not push pilots out by a full year
Quoted price will fit the realistic budget band once FX/VAT and setup are included
Hotel’s ADR/RevPAR data are accurate enough to baseline ROI despite revenue field gaps
gm.most_fragile_assumption: Owner will approve a recurring CHF 3k–5k commitment without a Swiss mid‑market reference and an enforceable ROI/exit guarantee.
gm.strongest_wedge: ROI‑tied 90‑day pilot with PMS/booking‑engine integration and Swiss hosting, backed by a comparable Swiss reference.
gm.strongest_blocker: Owner pushback on recurring spend and contract length without local proof and KPI guarantees.
gm.recommended_outreach_angle: Propose a Swiss‑hosted, PMS‑integrated 90‑day pilot at CHF 1.2k–1.8k/month with monthly opt‑out and an upgrade path only if KPIs are hit (+5pp direct‑share or +10% direct‑booking revenue within 6 months). Include a live dashboard demo, a Swiss 40–60 room reference, clear DPA/GDPR documents, and a weekly prioritized action plan tailored to a 49‑room team. Keep setup under 30 days, minimize staff time, and offer annual discount or performance pricing upon successful pilot.

key_assumptions: Current direct-booking share and web conversion baselines can be established within 2–4 weeks for credible attribution.
PMS/booking-engine connectors are available and can be implemented with light IT lift in ≤30 days.
Swiss Quality affiliation and any brand policies will not block a new vendor with Swiss/GDPR-compliant hosting.
Existing agency or tooling contracts allow adding/replacing services without break costs that derail ROI.
Small team has enough weekly bandwidth to action a prioritized plan without needing extra headcount.
Seasonality/approval cadence will allow a pilot window that isn’t distorted by extreme demand spikes or closures.
ADR data in dossier (CHF ~639) is an outlier; real RevPAR/occupancy will still support a modest marketing-software budget but not unchecked spend.
commercial.most_fragile_assumption: That direct-share uplift can be measured and credibly attributed within a short pilot despite missing baselines and potential integration/approval delays.
commercial.strongest_wedge: Low-risk, Swiss-hosted 60–90 day pilot tied to a clear direct-booking KPI with PMS attribution, delivering a weekly action plan that saves management/agency hours.
commercial.strongest_blocker: Owner/CFO insist on proven, attributable revenue impact and minimal integration risk before approving a recurring spend; procurement/brand policies may slow or block.
commercial.recommended_outreach_angle: Propose a Swiss-hosted, 60–90 day pilot at CHF 1.5–2.0k/month with monthly opt-out, performance-linked extension, and a clear success KPI (e.g., +6–8pp direct-share or +CHF X direct revenue vs. baseline). Include: PMS/booking-engine integration for attribution, weekly prioritized commercial actions (pricing/compset alerts + multilingual content briefs), and two Swiss references of similar size. Provide an ROI calculator upfront and commit to white-glove onboarding completed in ≤30 days. Time the pilot to an operationally calmer window (post-peak season) and offer an annual discount only after KPI is achieved.
commercial.canonical_deal_killer: No demonstrable uplift in direct bookings (or credible attribution path) within the agreed proof period.

merged.primary_wedge: Low-risk, Swiss-hosted 60–90 day pilot tied to a clear direct-booking KPI with PMS attribution, delivering a weekly action plan that saves management/agency hours.
merged.primary_blocker: Owner pushback on recurring spend and contract length without local proof and KPI guarantees.
merged.outreach_angle: Propose a Swiss‑hosted, PMS‑integrated 90‑day pilot at CHF 1.2k–1.8k/month with monthly opt‑out and an upgrade path only if KPIs are hit (+5pp direct‑share or +10% direct‑booking revenue within 6 months). Include a live dashboard demo, a Swiss 40–60 room reference, clear DPA/GDPR documents, and a weekly prioritized action plan tailored to a 49‑room team. Keep setup under 30 days, minimize staff time, and offer annual discount or performance pricing upon successful pilot.
disagreements: deal_killer: GM="no_pms_integration" vs CM="No demonstrable uplift in direct bookings (or credible attribution path) within the agreed proof period."

key_assumptions: Despite dossier revenue showing CHF 0, the hotel’s true annual room revenue is materially >CHF 3M given 43 rooms, ADR ~CHF 362, and typical luxury occupancy; owner will judge ROI against this scale.
GM/Head of Sales & Marketing have enough discretion to run a limited pilot (<=CHF 2k/month) without lengthy board approval.
A >=10% uplift in direct bookings within ~3 months is achievable if weekly actions are executed and attribution is in place.
PMS/CRS and booking‑engine integrations (and data export/ingest) can be completed within ~30 days without significant third‑party fees.
Swiss‑hosted/GDPR‑compliant data handling and Swiss luxury references are available from the vendor.
The small team can absorb a weekly action list without adding headcount and without displacing critical operations.
Existing agencies/tools will not materially resist or block adoption (no vested‑interest veto).
No binding brand/group vendor restrictions will prevent a pilot or require lengthy certification.
gm.most_fragile_assumption: Achieving and credibly attributing >=10% direct‑booking uplift within a 3‑month window for a mature 43‑room luxury property.
gm.strongest_wedge: Low‑risk 90‑day pilot tied to PMS/booking‑engine attribution that produces a single, prioritized weekly commercial action list (pricing/compset moves + DE/EN/IT content) with Swiss‑hosted GDPR compliance and Swiss luxury references—positioned as replacing fragmented agency hours and avoiding an extra hire.
gm.strongest_blocker: Owner/finance skepticism about ROI attribution and added operational burden, compounded by PMS/CRS integration lead times and potential agency resistance.
gm.recommended_outreach_angle: Lead with Swiss luxury references and a live dashboard demo that shows compset moves and how actions are generated. Propose a 90‑day, Swiss‑hosted pilot at CHF 1.5–2k/month, month‑to‑month, with white‑glove onboarding and confirmed PMS/BE data flow. Success metric: +10% direct bookings OR equivalent uplift in direct revenue plus replaced agency hours. Time the start to a shoulder period; commit to a 30‑minute weekly cadence and provide DE/EN/IT content packs for offers, events, and wellness to reduce workload.
gm.canonical_deal_killer: Failure to demonstrate attributable ROI on direct bookings/revenue within the pilot window or inability to satisfy Swiss‑hosted GDPR/data‑governance requirements.

key_assumptions: Two-way PMS/booking engine (and channel manager) integrations are feasible within 30–60 days with vendor cooperation
A conservative 5–10% uplift in direct channel revenue in 6 months is achievable through weekly, prioritized actions without adding headcount
The platform can replace or materially reduce some agency/consultant spend and manual spreadsheet time to fit the 1–2k/month budget band
Swiss/GDPR-compliant hosting and at least one credible Swiss luxury reference can be produced to satisfy owner/legal scrutiny
The owner/finance team will approve an operating expense for software mid-cycle if a low-risk pilot shows attribution
Recommendations will not create parity issues or require rate strategy changes that the GM/owner resist
The small team can consistently action the weekly recommendations during both high and shoulder seasons
commercial.most_fragile_assumption: Owner/Investor will approve a 3–5k/month recurring SaaS without airtight Swiss luxury references and a near-term, attributable payback
commercial.strongest_wedge: A Swiss-hosted, PMS-integrated 90-day pilot priced in the 1–2k/month band that replaces part of agency spend and produces a weekly, 3-step action list with attributable direct-revenue and OTA-commission savings
commercial.strongest_blocker: Owner/Investor veto on recurring SaaS (3–5k/month) without local luxury references and guaranteed, short-term payback; compounded by integration/legal reviews
commercial.recommended_outreach_angle: Lead with a low-friction, Swiss-hosted 90-day pilot anchored to their PMS/booking engine, priced in the 1–2k/month band with monthly opt-out. Show a live dashboard demo using their ADR/seasonality assumptions and an ROI calculator that ties three weekly prioritized actions to direct-channel revenue and OTA commission savings. Commit to white-glove onboarding, bilingual (EN/DE) content support, and provide at least one Swiss luxury reference. Position budget as a partial replacement of agency/consultant spend and manual time, not a net-new cost. Avoid overpromising 10%+; instead, propose conservative milestones (e.g., 5–7% direct uplift or specific parity wins) with owner-ready reporting.
commercial.canonical_deal_killer: No credible PMS/booking engine integration preventing attribution of actions to direct revenue uplift

merged.primary_wedge: A Swiss-hosted, PMS-integrated 90-day pilot priced in the 1–2k/month band that replaces part of agency spend and produces a weekly, 3-step action list with attributable direct-revenue and OTA-commission savings
merged.primary_blocker: Owner/finance skepticism about ROI attribution and added operational burden, compounded by PMS/CRS integration lead times and potential agency resistance.
merged.outreach_angle: Lead with Swiss luxury references and a live dashboard demo that shows compset moves and how actions are generated. Propose a 90‑day, Swiss‑hosted pilot at CHF 1.5–2k/month, month‑to‑month, with white‑glove onboarding and confirmed PMS/BE data flow. Success metric: +10% direct bookings OR equivalent uplift in direct revenue plus replaced agency hours. Time the start to a shoulder period; commit to a 30‑minute weekly cadence and provide DE/EN/IT content packs for offers, events, and wellness to reduce workload.
merged.deal_killer: Failure to demonstrate attributable ROI on direct bookings/revenue within the pilot window or inability to satisfy Swiss‑hosted GDPR/data‑governance requirements.
disagreements: deal_killer: GM="Failure to demonstrate attributable ROI on direct bookings/revenue within the pilot window or inability to satisfy Swiss‑hosted GDPR/data‑governance requirements." vs CM="No credible PMS/booking engine integration preventing attribution of actions to direct revenue uplift"

key_assumptions: We can stand up reliable enquiry-to-booking attribution for a contact-only workflow (web form, email, phone) within ~30–45 days, with Swiss-hosted data flows.
The GM/commercial lead has enough bandwidth and authority to action a weekly 5–8 item commercial to‑do list without adding headcount.
Low booking volumes and long lead times at a 25‑unit, very high‑ADR chalet can still yield a statistically credible signal of uplift within a 60–90 day pilot.
Owner will accept pilot-based attribution (including call/email matching) plus 1–2 Swiss luxury references as sufficient proof to approve ongoing fees.
Automation will be constrained to internal decision support and draft content so brand tone and bespoke service are never compromised.
gm.most_fragile_assumption: That we can deliver clean, auditable attribution from enquiry → booking in a contact-only flow quickly enough to prove ROI to the owner.
gm.strongest_wedge: A Swiss-hosted, 60-day shoulder-season pilot that proves enquiry→booking attribution for the contact-only flow and delivers weekly, GM-ready actions that lift peak-date yield with minimal team effort.
gm.strongest_blocker: Inability to produce clean, auditable attribution from contact enquiries to confirmed bookings; the owner will not approve recurring spend without this.
gm.recommended_outreach_angle: Lead with a low-friction, Swiss-hosted 60-day pilot scheduled for shoulder season. Commit to building bespoke connectors for the contact form, tracked emails, and call logs to attribute revenue, and preload a Zermatt luxury compset for peak-date alerts. Deliver a single weekly action email (pricing, offers, outreach targets) and keep guest-facing automation opt-in/approval-only to protect brand tone. Provide two Swiss luxury references, a clear ROI calculator, and performance-linked pricing options. Offer white-glove onboarding and a monthly opt-out after the pilot to de-risk owner sign-off.
gm.canonical_deal_killer: No reliable integration to track enquiries and attribute direct bookings (cannot prove revenue uplift).

key_assumptions: There is enough direct-enquiry volume in a 25-key, contact-only booking flow to measure a 10–15% conversion uplift within 3–6 months.
Lightweight integration or robust manual tagging can credibly attribute enquiry-to-booking outcomes without heavy IT lift.
Owner/Finance will accept a pilot with performance-linked terms rather than only CAPEX or agency-commission models.
Swiss/EU data residency and a couple of comparable Swiss luxury references will satisfy privacy/brand standards.
The small commercial/reservations team can execute weekly actions without adding headcount.
Existing agency/consultant can be co-opted (not displaced) for review intelligence/content without political blockage.
Seasonality still allows an off-season pilot window for onboarding and training.
commercial.most_fragile_assumption: Clean attribution to incremental direct revenue is achievable in a bespoke contact-only workflow without long, costly integration.
commercial.strongest_wedge: Attribution-first, Swiss-hosted 60–90 day pilot tailored to the contact-only flow, with performance-linked pricing and owner-grade ROI reporting.
commercial.strongest_blocker: Owner/Finance risk aversion driven by attribution ambiguity in the contact-only process; potential agency pushback.
commercial.recommended_outreach_angle: Propose an off-season, 60-day Swiss-hosted pilot focused on the contact-only journey. Deliver: (1) attribution plumbing via CRM/PMS light integration plus enquiry-tagging, (2) a weekly, owner-ready action pack (pricing/packaging, compset shifts, sentiment issues) with DE/EN/FR/IT content snippets, and (3) concierge/reservations coaching to capture tags without changing luxury tone. Commercials: CHF 2.5–3.0k/month pilot, performance-linked (milestone refund or fee credit if <10–15% uplift in contact-to-booking conversion or agreed CHF revenue delta), option for annual invoice post-pilot with modest discount, monthly opt-out during pilot. Include Swiss luxury references, GDPR/Swiss data residency, SLA on brand-safe messaging, and involve the current agency in review intelligence to avoid turf conflict. Target go-live in shoulder season to minimize disruption.
commercial.canonical_deal_killer: No credible attribution to incremental direct revenue in the contact-only booking flow within a short pilot.

merged.primary_wedge: Attribution-first, Swiss-hosted 60–90 day pilot tailored to the contact-only flow, with performance-linked pricing and owner-grade ROI reporting.
merged.primary_blocker: Inability to produce clean, auditable attribution from contact enquiries to confirmed bookings; the owner will not approve recurring spend without this.
merged.outreach_angle: Lead with a low-friction, Swiss-hosted 60-day pilot scheduled for shoulder season. Commit to building bespoke connectors for the contact form, tracked emails, and call logs to attribute revenue, and preload a Zermatt luxury compset for peak-date alerts. Deliver a single weekly action email (pricing, offers, outreach targets) and keep guest-facing automation opt-in/approval-only to protect brand tone. Provide two Swiss luxury references, a clear ROI calculator, and performance-linked pricing options. Offer white-glove onboarding and a monthly opt-out after the pilot to de-risk owner sign-off.
merged.deal_killer: No reliable integration to track enquiries and attribute direct bookings (cannot prove revenue uplift).
disagreements: deal_killer: GM="No reliable integration to track enquiries and attribute direct bookings (cannot prove revenue uplift)." vs CM="No credible attribution to incremental direct revenue in the contact-only booking flow within a short pilot."

key_assumptions: A 10%+ uplift in direct room revenue is achievable for an 18-room, very high-ADR boutique within 3–6 months without large incremental media spend or channel mix changes.
The small team can consistently action 3–5 weekly commercial tasks alongside peak-season operations.
PMS/booking-engine integration is available, stable, and low-friction for the hotel’s current stack.
Owner/Finance will approve new recurring opex if pilot evidence is strong and risk-limited.
Swiss/GDPR-compliant hosting and white-glove onboarding are included without pushing price beyond the viable band for a micro-luxury property.
gm.most_fragile_assumption: That a 10%+ direct revenue uplift can be delivered and verified within 3–6 months for an 18-room ultra‑high‑ADR alpine hotel without significant additional marketing spend.
gm.strongest_wedge: Weekly decision engine + review intelligence tailored to a tiny luxury team, directly tied to measurable direct-booking uplift.
gm.strongest_blocker: Owner/Finance insist on audited, local proof of net direct revenue uplift and are averse to a CHF 3k–5k recurring commitment.
gm.recommended_outreach_angle: Propose a 90-day shoulder-season pilot at CHF 1.5–2k/month with Swiss/GDPR hosting, native PMS/BE integration, and white-glove 2-week onboarding. Commit to 3–5 prioritized weekly actions, couples/wellness package tests, and compset-tuned pricing prompts. Make success criteria explicit (≥10% direct revenue uplift or agreed KPI bundle) with refund/credit and monthly opt-out. Bring one Swiss luxury reference and a live dashboard demo up front.
gm.canonical_deal_killer: No credible, local proof that hotels of similar size see net direct revenue uplift within 3–6 months.

key_assumptions: A short pilot can be scheduled outside peak ski weeks to reduce seasonality noise and show signal.
Lightweight measurement (booking‑engine tags/GA4 + later PMS tie-in) will be accepted for attribution if full PMS integration slips.
Owner/finance will tolerate a performance‑linked or capped pilot fee even if they resist high recurring SaaS.
Swiss/GDPR hosting assurances and at least one Swiss luxury reference can be produced early.
The small team can action a weekly plan (pricing/packages/content/reviews) without adding headcount.
Multilingual (EN/FR, optionally DE/IT) content and review intelligence will improve conversion without risky UX changes.
Existing agency/manual work can be partially replaced to free budget in the 1–2k band.
Tooling will respect brand tone and avoid any perception of automated guest‑facing messaging that feels off‑brand.
commercial.most_fragile_assumption: We can credibly attribute ≥10% direct‑revenue uplift within 3 months for an 18‑room, highly seasonal luxury property.
commercial.strongest_wedge: Performance‑linked 60–90 day Swiss‑hosted pilot focused on shoulder‑season direct revenue and OTA commission reduction, delivering a simple weekly action plan (reviews, multilingual content, targeted offers) that replaces agency/manual time; start with light tracking and upgrade to PMS integration once results are visible.
commercial.strongest_blocker: Owner/Finance skepticism about recurring SaaS and attribution on an 18‑room, seasonal luxury asset; plus potential PMS/booking‑engine integration lead times.
commercial.recommended_outreach_angle: Lead with a Swiss‑hosted, GDPR‑clean 60–90 day pilot scheduled post‑peak. Scope phase 1 to review intelligence + multilingual content + compset nudges, delivering a one‑page weekly action plan the team can execute in under 2 hours. Measurement: unique landing/offer codes + GA4/booking‑engine tags; commit to adding PMS integration mid‑pilot if vendor slots allow. Commercials: CHF 1.5k/month pilot or CHF 1k base + performance component; monthly opt‑out; owner pack including 1 Swiss luxury reference, ROI calculator, and a clear attribution plan. Avoid aggressive price automation; emphasize brand‑safe copy and time savings vs agency/manual work.
commercial.canonical_deal_killer: Cannot prove or credibly attribute direct‑booking uplift during the pilot, or failure to achieve workable PMS/booking‑engine tracking for measurement.

merged.primary_wedge: Performance‑linked 60–90 day Swiss‑hosted pilot focused on shoulder‑season direct revenue and OTA commission reduction, delivering a simple weekly action plan (reviews, multilingual content, targeted offers) that replaces agency/manual time; start with light tracking and upgrade to PMS integration once results are visible.
merged.primary_blocker: Owner/Finance insist on audited, local proof of net direct revenue uplift and are averse to a CHF 3k–5k recurring commitment.
merged.outreach_angle: Propose a 90-day shoulder-season pilot at CHF 1.5–2k/month with Swiss/GDPR hosting, native PMS/BE integration, and white-glove 2-week onboarding. Commit to 3–5 prioritized weekly actions, couples/wellness package tests, and compset-tuned pricing prompts. Make success criteria explicit (≥10% direct revenue uplift or agreed KPI bundle) with refund/credit and monthly opt-out. Bring one Swiss luxury reference and a live dashboard demo up front.
merged.deal_killer: No credible, local proof that hotels of similar size see net direct revenue uplift within 3–6 months.
disagreements: deal_killer: GM="No credible, local proof that hotels of similar size see net direct revenue uplift within 3–6 months." vs CM="Cannot prove or credibly attribute direct‑booking uplift during the pilot, or failure to achieve workable PMS/booking‑engine tracking for measurement."
